mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically boosting present valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0 percent, tight labor market conditions accelerate wage growth and consumer spending, driving corporate earnings revisions upward and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE is below 5.0, or the predictive Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in industrial production and contraction in real residential construction signals a capital-intensive growth phase that outpaces housing-driven inflation, compressing real yields and lifting equity valuations over the following month.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative directional accuracy below 50% over a 5-year out-of-sample test, or the interaction term coefficient in a multivariate regression becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During aggressive monetary tightening cycles, a sustained decline in real consumer credit balances signals tightening financial conditions that rapidly compress equity multiples and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of observations during identified tightening phases, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for VIX levels.

mechanism_narrative: When real consumer credit growth exceeds 4.0 percent year-over-year, excessive leverage signals impending debt servicing stress that triggers earnings downgrades and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the out-of-sample Sharpe ratio falls below zero over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained upward trend in the industrial production index reflects expanding capacity utilization and healthy aggregate supply, which supports corporate profit margins and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns show no statistical difference between rising and falling INDPRO periods (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous occurrence of rising employment payrolls and a federal funds rate above 4.5 percent indicates an overheating economy where wage-push inflation forces prolonged restrictive policy, ultimately depressing equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative directional accuracy below 50% over a 5-year out-of-sample test, or the interaction term coefficient in a multivariate regression becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When real consumer credit growth decelerates below 2.0 percent, it signals a contraction in household purchasing power that rapidly transmits to corporate revenue misses and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the predictive Rank IC drops below 0.01 over a 2-year rolling window.
